title: toxicodendron wallichii var. microcarpum
intro: Toxicodendron wallichii var. microcarpum, also known as the Himalayan poison ivy, is a plant species that belongs to the Anacardiaceae family, characterized by its shiny green leaves and yellowish-green flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of urushiol, a toxic resin. The health benefits of Toxicodendron wallichii var. microcarpum include its ability to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and reduce fever. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of a decoction, ointment, or powder, and it is commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various ailments.
